title: Approval Expiration Policy
description: Expiration semantics for all governance approval windows. Expired approvals fail closed. Expiration state is deterministic from (issuedAt, kind) alone, ensuring replay reconstruction reproduces the same expiration decisions.

dual-auth.description: Dual-authorization window — two distinct operators must approve within 1 hour

operator-action.description: Single operator action authorization window — 30 minutes

invariants: Expired approvals MUST throw ApprovalExpiredError (fail closed — no silent degradation)
Expiration is deterministic: given same issuedAt and kind, expiresAt is always equal
replayExpirationChain produces equal records from equal inputs
Boundary: an approval evaluated at exactly expiresAt is considered expired
Expiration evidence is append-only — expiredAt field is set once and never mutated
closed_gap: INIT-2.4.3: Approval expiration was implicit in dual-auth TTL only. Now all governance approval windows have explicit, deterministic expiration semantics with fail-closed enforcement.
